quick_facts.label: What this node represents
quick_facts.value: A Bachelor of Science in Computer Science awarded by a university outside India. The exact award structure, credit total and graduation requirements are set by the university; for example, Stanford’s BS includes mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, CS core/depth and a senior project alongside general undergraduate requirements.

quick_facts.label: Academic entry baseline
quick_facts.value: Applicants normally apply from a recognised senior-secondary qualification and must meet institution- and programme-specific prerequisites. As one current international-admission example, the University of Toronto requires senior/Grade 12 English and states that calculus is required for most mathematics, science and computer science programmes.

quick_facts.label: IELTS and SAT role
quick_facts.value: IELTS Academic is designed to assess English for academic study, but each university sets its own accepted tests, exemptions and minimum scores. SAT policy is also university-specific: the University of Toronto says applicants who have taken the SAT or ACT may submit scores for consideration, rather than presenting it as a universal requirement.

quick_facts.label: Recognition in India
quick_facts.value: For an Indian graduate who needs a formal equivalence certificate, UGC’s 2025 foreign-qualification regulations require, among other conditions, an award from an institution duly recognised in its home country and study completed under that institution’s prescribed standards. Qualifications obtained through franchising arrangements are not considered for equivalence under these regulations.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative duration
quick_facts.value: There is no single worldwide duration for this node; confirm the specific programme’s normal length and progression rules. As a Canadian reference point, University of Toronto fee documentation describes the normal length of its degree programmes as four years for students not taking an International Foundation Program.

sections.heading: Eligibility and preparation
paragraphs: Start with the target university’s country-specific requirements for the Indian school qualification being presented (for example, CBSE, CISCE/ISC, state board, IB or A levels), then check the Computer Science prerequisites separately. A current University of Toronto example requires senior/Grade 12 English for all applicants and indicates that calculus is required for most mathematics, science and computer science programmes.

English-language proof is not automatic merely because the degree is taught in English. IELTS Academic is intended to measure readiness for study in an academic environment, but the accepting institution determines its own score threshold and any exemption rules. Applicants should therefore use the university’s current admissions page—not a generic score target—as the controlling requirement.

SAT scores can be useful only where the chosen university permits, recommends or requires them. For example, University of Toronto permits applicants who have written the SAT or ACT to submit the scores for assessment. This supports the node’s “may consider SAT” description, but it should not be read as a rule for every foreign university.
bullets: Keep final and predicted/term marks, subject syllabi where requested, passport details, and official test-score reporting information ready.
Verify mathematics prerequisites early; Computer Science programmes may distinguish between calculus, advanced mathematics, statistics and computer science subjects.
Check whether the institution requires scores to be sent directly by the test provider and whether the policy differs by faculty, campus or intake.

sections.heading: Duration and study pattern
paragraphs: Treat duration as a programme-specific fact, not a property of every overseas BSc in Computer Science. One four-year undergraduate model is illustrated by University of Toronto documentation, which states that the normal length of a degree programme is four years for students who do not take an International Foundation Program. The final completion time can also depend on entry pathway, transfer credit, course load, co-op or placement structure, and academic progression rules.

Before applying, compare the award title, full-time duration, required credits, campus location, teaching mode, foundation-year implications and whether a work-integrated-learning option adds time. These details matter both for budgeting and for any later equivalence assessment in India.
bullets: Do not assume that a three-year, four-year, honours, integrated-master’s, transfer-entry or foundation-route programme has the same duration or credit structure.
If future Indian equivalence is important, retain official transcripts, programme regulations, credit information and evidence of the awarding institution’s recognition.

sections.heading: What the curriculum commonly covers
paragraphs: A Computer Science bachelor’s curriculum is broader than programming alone. Stanford’s published BS requirements provide a useful official example: its CS major combines mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, technology-and-society study, CS core and depth coursework, and a senior project; its listed topic areas include artificial intelligence, computational biology, computer engineering, human-computer interaction, information, systems, theory and visual computing.

This is an example rather than a universal template. Compare the target university’s current degree requirements for compulsory mathematics, programming foundations, algorithms, systems, software engineering, electives, project/capstone work, general education, and internship or co-op opportunities before deciding between programmes.
bullets: Look for the balance between theoretical foundations and applied software-development work.
Check whether the degree has a required capstone, research project, lab component or industry placement.
Review the latest course catalogue because elective menus and programme rules can change after admission.

sections.heading: Application and admission process
paragraphs: Apply through the process specified by each university and submit every required academic record by that programme’s published deadline. University of Toronto notes that international applicants may receive conditional offers based on prior-year and in-term results, with final completion of required senior-level courses still required.

A practical order is: shortlist recognised universities and specific Computer Science programmes; verify academic prerequisites and English-language rules; decide whether SAT submission is applicable; submit the application and supporting documents; then complete the destination country’s student-immigration process only after receiving an offer. For the United States, an SEVP-certified school may issue Form I-20 after admission, which a prospective F or M student uses to apply for a visa.

For Indian students planning to use the qualification in India, assess the institutional and programme status before paying a deposit. UGC’s 2025 framework requires the foreign institution to be duly recognised in its home country for equivalence purposes and excludes franchising arrangements from consideration.
bullets: Use the university’s own admissions page for current deadlines, testing rules, document formats and application fees.
Do not assume SAT, IELTS, transfer credit, scholarships or visa rules are shared across countries or universities.
Save offer letters, fee receipts, course descriptions, transcripts and the final degree certificate for immigration, employment, postgraduate applications and potential equivalence requests.

sections.heading: Costs and financial planning
paragraphs: Costs are highly country-, university-, campus- and year-specific. Budget for tuition, compulsory institutional charges, health cover, accommodation, food, books/equipment, local transport, flights, visa costs and contingency funds; do not rely on part-time work as the primary financing plan.

As dated fee context only, University of Toronto Scarborough’s 2026–27 viewbook lists international Computer Science tuition of CAD 63,570 in Year 1 and CAD 65,480 in Years 2–5, while separately listing books/supplies and university health insurance as additional estimated costs. This is one institution’s published context, not a typical price or a forecast for another university.

Immigration financial evidence can be material. For Canada, the government states that study-permit applicants must show they can pay tuition, living expenses and transportation without working in Canada. For applications on or after September 1, 2025, the listed first-year living-expense amount for one person outside Quebec is CAD 22,895, excluding tuition and transport; the government states these figures are updated annually.
bullets: Request the target university’s official international fee schedule for the intended entry year and verify whether the listed amount is annual, per term or per credit.
Separate refundable and non-refundable deposits from total tuition.
Check scholarship eligibility, renewal conditions, currency-conversion exposure and the evidence-of-funds rules for the chosen destination.

sections.heading: After graduation: further study, work and Indian use of the degree
paragraphs: A completed overseas BS in Computer Science can support applications to postgraduate study or employment, but each receiving university, employer, licensing body and immigration authority sets its own criteria. Where formal comparability in India is needed, the graduate can consider the UGC foreign-qualification equivalence route and should ensure the award and institution meet the applicable conditions.

Post-study work permission is country-specific and must not be assumed from the degree alone. In the United States, the Department of Homeland Security notes that eligible F-1 students may apply to USCIS for post-completion Optional Practical Training, and that STEM OPT is governed by separate extension rules and a formal training-plan requirement. Students should verify the applicable rules at graduation, because immigration policy can change.

Keep a complete academic record: degree certificate, final transcript, course and credit information, institutional recognition evidence, and any placement/project records. These documents help with postgraduate admission, employer screening, immigration applications and an equivalence request.
bullets: For India-based postgraduate or public-sector use, ask the receiving institution or recruiter whether an equivalence certificate is required before applying.
For overseas work plans, consult the destination government’s current student and graduate-work rules rather than relying on university marketing.
For professional programmes outside Computer Science, check whether a separate statutory regulator—not UGC—controls recognition.
